reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the prescribing practitioner to consider a low-microgram chronic frame for oxytocin, with the experts' verbatim ceiling at 50 mcg per dose. Hunter (199e4c887351c463 2025-10-14): "Dose: 50 mcg per dose, 3-4× daily" with "Route: Intranasal OR subcutaneous (Hunter likes both — intranasal more convenient for multiple daily doses)" and "Timing: 15-30 min before meals"; Taylor (7pMGxNXhRsA 00:10:47.800): "50 micrograms um subcutaneously" and (7pMGxNXhRsA 00:11:01.200): "would just rather inject than do a nasal spray." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh route preference (nasal vs subq) against the patient's GI sensitivity and the use-case (fat-loss recomp vs social/bonding vs labor-adjacent).
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Not for pregnancy/labor self-experimentation: Trevor (JBWlwYL8AOo 00:17:54.559): "Oxytocin which raises intracellular calcium which I just talked about in the uterus to drive labor." Hunter (199e4c887351c463 2025-10-14): "On/off cycling = prudent (like most hormones)."
- GI flush risk if dosed non-fasted: Taylor (7pMGxNXhRsA 00:11:50.910): "taking this peptide fasted where you have no calories in your system and I would wait to take it until after you make your first bve movement of the day." Taylor (7pMGxNXhRsA 00:12:14.000) describes taking it with calories: "basically end up spending 30 minutes like in the bathroom so not really the experience I was wanting to have with this peptide."
- Nasal vs subq side-effect split: Taylor (7pMGxNXhRsA 00:11:09.000): "the nasal spray affected affects my GI system more" while (7pMGxNXhRsA 00:11:14.279): "injecting it affected more of" "more of my emotional um cognitive health."
- Cycling: Hunter (199e4c887351c463 2025-10-14): "Duration: 8-12 weeks on, then re-evaluate. Cycle 4-6 weeks off."
- Hunter himself doesn't run it continuously: Hunter (tcaX0xrCMR4 01:08:52.319): "Like oxytocin, I don't need it all the time."
